title: Male lips: How to create the perfect lips for male patients
filename: Male lips： How to create the perfect lips for male patients.en.vtt
excerpt: So how do you create the perfect lip treatment for a male patient [Music] the first thing to say about this is not all males are the same it's not really about being male or female it's about the patient's stories you've got to figure out what is it that this patient wants do the…

segments.text: so how do you create the perfect lip treatment for a male patient [Music] the first thing to say about this is not all males are the same it's not really about being male or female it's about the patient's stories you've got to figure out what is it that this patient wants do they want restoration do they want to look more feminine which is relatively common a degree of femininity it's not that they're transgender or anything like that but that it's a kind of um handsome younger face um but it can be hinting on femininity you just need to get it clear if you're not clear what the goal is you might do the wrong treatment now the nature of full lips are a feminine feature if you look at the classic female proportions they in they involve larger lips than in male patients so there is no large male lip treatment the larger you make someone's lip the more feminine it is getting in fact my children picked us up very recently and they are

not trained in aesthetics we don't talk about that kind of stuff over dinner um and they saw they were joking around you know when you fold your lower lip down and put your tongue out it makes your lips look bigger and like the youngest one who's five said to my oldest one you look like a girl and i thought well isn't that interesting that he's figured out that larger lips are a feminine proportion so there you can certainly do lip treatment i've done many lip treatments on men they're usually restorative or adding a little bit of volume you know trying to get proportions a bit better but full voluptuous lips are a female characteristic so by their very nature if you're treating a male who wants a masculine outcome it tends to be a milder treatment longer flatter lips uh less curves you're not trying to enhance beauty they're not voluptuous um so you've got to think about them fundamentally different differently and in many ways they are therefore a bit easier because you're usually trying to create less of a difference um to make the patient happy so hope that helps

it's not that they're transgender or anything like that but that it's a kind of um handsome younger face um but it can be hinting on femininity you just need to get it clear if you're not clear what the goal is you might do the wrong treatment now the nature of full lips are a feminine feature if you look at the classic female proportions they in they involve larger lips than in male patients so there is no large male lip treatment the larger you make someone's lip the more feminine it is getting in fact my children picked us up very recently and they are not trained in aesthetics we don't youngest one who's five said to my oldest one you look like a girl and i thought well isn't that interesting that he's figured out that larger lips are a feminine proportion so there you can certainly do lip treatment i've done many lip treatments on men they're usually restorative or adding a little bit of volume you know trying to get proportions a bit better but full voluptuous lips are a female characteristic so by their very nature if you're treating a male who wants a masculine outcome it tends to be a milder treatment longer flatter lips uh less curves you're not trying to enhance beauty they're not voluptuous um so you've got to think about them fundamentally different differently and in many ways they are therefore a bit easier because you're usually trying to create less of a difference um to make the patient happy so hope that helps
